headline: LLM sentiment signals mature beyond simple polarity (dispersion and uncertainty are the real predictors); momentum alpha decays hyperbolically faster than expected post-ETF crowding; cross-asset macro features beat technicals in regime ML — three actionable threads for the existing slate.

themes.theme: Multi-Dimensional News Sentiment: Dispersion + Uncertainty Beat Polarity
themes.summary: Two 2026 papers converge on the same counter-intuitive result: net positive/negative tone is a weak predictor, but cross-article DISAGREEMENT (sentiment dispersion) and UNCERTAINTY language are the dominant alpha signals. GDELT + FinBERT + XGBoost on macro news achieves Sharpe ratios of 5.87 (EUR/USD), 4.65 (USD/JPY), and 4.65 (10-yr Treasuries) out-of-sample 2017–Apr 2025, with sentiment dispersion as the top SHAP feature (arxiv 2505.16136). For WTI crude oil, GPT-4o intensity mean and FinBERT polarity standard deviation — not net polarity — yield IC 0.2283 and AUC 0.6515 vs 0.5694 baseline (arxiv 2603.11408). Both papers flag hedging language and ambiguity as economically meaningful dimensions that simple FinBERT scores discard.
themes.implication: fits existing: news-reactive — replace or augment the morning-brief polarity score with dispersion (variance of scores across articles) and uncertainty-word density; sharp cross-source disagreement is itself the signal, not the direction of the consensus

themes.theme: Hyperbolic Momentum Alpha Decay Accelerated by Factor ETF Crowding
themes.summary: A late-2025 paper derives a hyperbolic decay model alpha(t) = K / (1 + λt) that fits US equity momentum significantly better than linear (R²=0.65 vs 0.51) or exponential (R²=0.61) alternatives. The alarming finding: factor ETF AUM growth correlates ρ=−0.63 with crowding acceleration post-2015, and out-of-sample the model over-predicts remaining momentum alpha (model forecast 0.30, realised only 0.15). Mechanical factors (momentum, short-term reversal) crowd far faster than judgment-based ones (value, quality); this asymmetry is robust across sub-periods (arxiv 2512.11913).
themes.implication: fits existing: momentum-trader — momentum's effective holding period has compressed post-2015 due to ETF crowding; backtest whether tighter exits (1–2 days vs current 3–5) or a rolling factor-ETF-flow crowding proxy as a position-size gate recovers the Sharpe

themes.theme: Volume-Price Interactions and Multi-Period Mean Reversion Survive Strict Double-Selection LASSO
themes.summary: Testing 168 short-term Alpha191 signals against 151 fundamental control factors via double-selection LASSO on S&P 500 constituents (2002–2022, monthly rebalance), only 17 survive t > 2.0. Dominant survivors are volume-price interactions: Multi-Period Mean Reversion Ratio (79 bps/month, t = 3.68), 20-day cumulative On-Balance Volume (51 bps/month, t = 3.68), and overnight gap return (41 bps/month, t = 2.46). Counter-intuitively, the signals strengthen monotonically from 1-day to 21-day horizons — ruling out microstructure-noise explanations and suggesting persistent behavioural or liquidity premia (arxiv 2601.06499).
themes.implication: spawn-candidate: mean-reverter-v2 — add 20-day On-Balance Volume and overnight gap return as entry-confirmation filters layered on top of the existing RSI<30 + SMA20-distance signals; these two survive the strictest empirical controls the literature has applied to short-term technical factors

themes.theme: Cross-Asset Macro Features (Yield Curve, Gold/Equity Ratio) Dominate Technicals in Regime ML
themes.summary: A 2026 walk-forward validated LightGBM study (Pagliaro, Electronics 15(6) 1334) shows via SHAP that yield-curve slope and gold/equity ratio contribute more predictive power than MACD, RSI, or Bollinger Bands for high-beta equities; BTC/equity cross-asset correlation adds incremental independent signal. The model autonomously learns mean-reversion logic in bear regimes and momentum logic in bull regimes. A companion arxiv paper combining FinBERT sentiment + XGBoost + a volatility-regime filter reported 135.49% cumulative return over 24 months vs ~40% for SPY in the same window (Sharpe not disclosed; arxiv 2601.19504).
themes.implication: fits existing: macro-aligned — extend the current yield-curve/DXY regime model to include gold/equity ratio as an explicit input; SHAP evidence that it dominates traditional technicals is strong enough to justify direct addition before any architectural change

themes.theme: Systematic Factor Models Signal Momentum + Energy/Materials Overweight in Current Macro Regime
themes.summary: Man Group's systematic quant team (May 2026 outlook) identifies the current environment — steepening yield curves, healthy equity markets, strong precious metals — as 'favourably disposed towards the Momentum factor, and to a lesser extent Quality, Profitability, and Growth.' Unexpectedly, their regime model gives Energy and Basic Materials a positive signal. Separately, the valuation risk flag is specific: >25% of MSCI World weight now exceeds 10× EV/Sales, above dot-com peaks, while US large-cap forward earnings are near +1σ and Japan is near −0.8σ (Man Group, 2026-through-systematic-lens).
themes.implication: fits existing: sector-rotator — current regime signal supports overweighting Energy and Basic Materials sector ETFs and trimming high-multiple tech exposure within the relative-strength rotation model; consistent with the macro-aligned regime read and the momentum-crowding finding above

body_md:
## LLM sentiment signals mature beyond simple polarity (dispersion and uncertainty are the real predictors); momentum alpha decays hyperbolically faster than expected post-ETF crowding; cross-asset macro features beat technicals in regime ML — three actionable threads for the existing slate.

### Multi-Dimensional News Sentiment: Dispersion + Uncertainty Beat Polarity

Two 2026 papers converge on the same counter-intuitive result: net positive/negative tone is a weak predictor, but cross-article DISAGREEMENT (sentiment dispersion) and UNCERTAINTY language are the dominant alpha signals. GDELT + FinBERT + XGBoost on macro news achieves Sharpe ratios of 5.87 (EUR/USD), 4.65 (USD/JPY), and 4.65 (10-yr Treasuries) out-of-sample 2017–Apr 2025, with sentiment dispersion as the top SHAP feature (arxiv 2505.16136). For WTI crude oil, GPT-4o intensity mean and FinBERT polarity standard deviation — not net polarity — yield IC 0.2283 and AUC 0.6515 vs 0.5694 baseline (arxiv 2603.11408). Both papers flag hedging language and ambiguity as economically meaningful dimensions that simple FinBERT scores discard.

**Implication:** fits existing: news-reactive — replace or augment the morning-brief polarity score with dispersion (variance of scores across articles) and uncertainty-word density; sharp cross-source disagreement is itself the signal, not the direction of the consensus

### Hyperbolic Momentum Alpha Decay Accelerated by Factor ETF Crowding

A late-2025 paper derives a hyperbolic decay model alpha(t) = K / (1 + λt) that fits US equity momentum significantly better than linear (R²=0.65 vs 0.51) or exponential (R²=0.61) alternatives. The alarming finding: factor ETF AUM growth correlates ρ=−0.63 with crowding acceleration post-2015, and out-of-sample the model over-predicts remaining momentum alpha (model forecast 0.30, realised only 0.15). Mechanical factors (momentum, short-term reversal) crowd far faster than judgment-based ones (value, quality); this asymmetry is robust across sub-periods (arxiv 2512.11913).

**Implication:** fits existing: momentum-trader — momentum's effective holding period has compressed post-2015 due to ETF crowding; backtest whether tighter exits (1–2 days vs current 3–5) or a rolling factor-ETF-flow crowding proxy as a position-size gate recovers the Sharpe

### Volume-Price Interactions and Multi-Period Mean Reversion Survive Strict Double-Selection LASSO

Testing 168 short-term Alpha191 signals against 151 fundamental control factors via double-selection LASSO on S&P 500 constituents (2002–2022, monthly rebalance), only 17 survive t > 2.0. Dominant survivors are volume-price interactions: Multi-Period Mean Reversion Ratio (79 bps/month, t = 3.68), 20-day cumulative On-Balance Volume (51 bps/month, t = 3.68), and overnight gap return (41 bps/month, t = 2.46). Counter-intuitively, the signals strengthen monotonically from 1-day to 21-day horizons — ruling out microstructure-noise explanations and suggesting persistent behavioural or liquidity premia (arxiv 2601.06499).

**Implication:** spawn-candidate: mean-reverter-v2 — add 20-day On-Balance Volume and overnight gap return as entry-confirmation filters layered on top of the existing RSI<30 + SMA20-distance signals; these two survive the strictest empirical controls the literature has applied to short-term technical factors

### Cross-Asset Macro Features (Yield Curve, Gold/Equity Ratio) Dominate Technicals in Regime ML

A 2026 walk-forward validated LightGBM study (Pagliaro, Electronics 15(6) 1334) shows via SHAP that yield-curve slope and gold/equity ratio contribute more predictive power than MACD, RSI, or Bollinger Bands for high-beta equities; BTC/equity cross-asset correlation adds incremental independent signal. The model autonomously learns mean-reversion logic in bear regimes and momentum logic in bull regimes. A companion arxiv paper combining FinBERT sentiment + XGBoost + a volatility-regime filter reported 135.49% cumulative return over 24 months vs ~40% for SPY in the same window (Sharpe not disclosed; arxiv 2601.19504).

**Implication:** fits existing: macro-aligned — extend the current yield-curve/DXY regime model to include gold/equity ratio as an explicit input; SHAP evidence that it dominates traditional technicals is strong enough to justify direct addition before any architectural change

### Systematic Factor Models Signal Momentum + Energy/Materials Overweight in Current Macro Regime

Man Group's systematic quant team (May 2026 outlook) identifies the current environment — steepening yield curves, healthy equity markets, strong precious metals — as 'favourably disposed towards the Momentum factor, and to a lesser extent Quality, Profitability, and Growth.' Unexpectedly, their regime model gives Energy and Basic Materials a positive signal. Separately, the valuation risk flag is specific: >25% of MSCI World weight now exceeds 10× EV/Sales, above dot-com peaks, while US large-cap forward earnings are near +1σ and Japan is near −0.8σ (Man Group, 2026-through-systematic-lens).

**Implication:** fits existing: sector-rotator — current regime signal supports overweighting Energy and Basic Materials sector ETFs and trimming high-multiple tech exposure within the relative-strength rotation model; consistent with the macro-aligned regime read and the momentum-crowding finding above

### Sources

- https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.16136
- https://arxiv.org/html/2603.11408v2
- https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.11913
- https://arxiv.org/html/2601.06499
- https://harbourfronttechnologies.wordpress.com/2026/04/29/regime-aware-trading-strategies-with-machine-learning/
- https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.19504
- https://www.man.com/insights/2026-through-systematic-lens
